page: OVHcloud c3-128 is a compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. The virtualization layer is managed via KVM.

In multi-core processing benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores. Memory performance is also strong, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth placing in the top 10% and large-block read/write bandwidth in the top 25%. The server excels in parallelized tasks, as evidenced by top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with clang, ray tracing, and image processing.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and high core density, the server offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that require predictable, non-shared CPU resources without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, high-throughput Redis database operations, and static web serving. However, with only 2.0 GB of RAM per core, it may present a resource tradeoff for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference tasks that exceed its memory capacity.
description: The OVHcloud c3-128 is a compute-optimized server utilizing a KVM hypervisor and an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It delivers 64 dedicated physical cores (64 vCPUs) at 2.3 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on an x86_64 architecture, the server features an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong single-core performance. This hardware profile is highly efficient for parallelized compute workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput database operations like Redis, though the 2.0 GB per core memory ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-128 is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-128 is a compute-optimized KVM server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD for high-performance workloads.
tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core compute platform with NVMe storage, engineered for highly parallelized workloads and intensive multi-threaded processing.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores running at 2.3 GHz.
128 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
400 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and software compilation performance.
Optimized for parallel processing, CI/CD pipelines, and high-throughput databases.
